denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 02/21/2026
Aetna Choice® POS II (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W100229651          Group #: GRP-1029
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-107994
Claim #: CLM-2026-189538
Date of Service (requested): 02/01/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Provider of record

Dear Plan Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 02/03/2026 for the requested service
Diagnosis (as submitted): Not itemized in this notice
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria.
Clinical policy applied: pediatric imaging criteria (ages 0–17) were applied in reviewing this request. CPB 0129 under Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) and InterQual® acute-care criteria

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in the applicable coverage policy and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), including utilization-management provisions. The information on file does not demonstrate that the requested service is medically necessary for this member at this time. The service is denied because plan criteria were not met. Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy utilization-management requirements for the benefit category under review. The record did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W100229651) and reference UM-2026-107994. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination.

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

Aetna Medical Management
Utilization Management Department
clinical_context: The 34-year-old member has Ovarian cyst, persistent 6 cm (N83.202). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Laparoscopic cystectomy recommended as the next appropriate step. Simple cyst 6.2 cm persistent ×3 cycles; pain with intercourse.
